explanation: 该论文关注城市空气质量改善的多层次治理，涉及空气污染监测和来源分析，与大气环境遥感技术中的空气质量监测和成分分析密切相关。
authors: Guus J.M. Velders;Andreas Eisold;Philippe Thunis;Gregor Kiesewetter;Martin Lutz;Rob Maas;Maaike Lammerts-Huitema;Martine Ouwersloot
title: Multi-level governance for improving air quality in cities in the UNECE region: a science policy perspective
abstract:
•Cities offers a unique opportunity to reduce air pollution related health impacts. •Dominant sources differ from city to city and from pollutant to pollutant. •Multi-level governance is needed to improve the air quality in cities. •Measures exist and offer synergies to other environmental and policy objectives. 
 Air pollution is the most harmful environmental risk factor to human health. Cities with high population density and associated high levels of air pollution offer a unique opportunity to significantly improve public health. The major sources of air pollution in cities differ from city to city and from pollutant to pollutant. In most cities combustion from residential heating or transport are the most relevant local sources of primary particulate matter and nitrogen dioxide. Other significant sources lie outside cities. The diversity of sources and pollutants means that no single government body can implement measures on its own to sufficiently improve the air quality to meet the air quality guideline levels of the WHO. Cross-governmental collaboration is also needed, because measures that reduce emissions in a wider region could deliver greater health benefits, by reducing the average exposure, than local measures targeting hotspots. Local information on sources as well as air quality monitoring and modelling are essential to assess which sectors are most relevant for each city. The most effective combination of policies to improve the air quality depends on the local context, the pollutant and its sources, but in all cases collaboration across government levels is essential. We give examples of how these levels of air quality management interact, which sources are most relevant and which pollutant-specific features should be considered in multi-level governance. Synergies across different policy areas, notably between climate, transport and air quality policies, offer various benefits among other environmental issues, e.g. urban planning and noise pollution.

explanation: 该论文研究了印度尼西亚热带沿海城市的空气质量预测，使用了机器学习技术（如Random Forest/XGBoost）和气象特征工程，涉及PM2.5等污染物的监测和预测，属于大气环境遥感技术领域，特别是空气质量监测方面。
authors: Asif Awaludin;Nani Cholianawati;Listi Restu Triani;Asri Indrawati;Ginaldi Ari Nugroho;Prawira Yudha Kombara;Sany Indra Putra;Aufa Zalfarani Saprudin;Tiin Sinatra;Eka Dian Pusfitasari;Ibnu Fathrio;Halimurrahman;Aisya Nafiisyanti;Dyah Aries Tanti;Dipo Yudhatama;Rizky Tazkia Arethusya Harijanto
title: Process-informed machine learning for interpretable air-quality prediction in tropical coastal cities of Sulawesi, Indonesia
abstract:
•Process-informed ML improved pollutant prediction, withR2reaching 0.85. •Robust CCM links were found for rainfall–PM2.5and rainfall–PM10. •SHAP revealed city-specific reliance on particulate, gaseous, and meteorological predictors. 
 Air-quality studies in tropical regions have focused mainly on highly polluted megacities, whereas pollutant variability in lower- to moderate-pollution regional urban environments remains less well understood. This study developed an interpretable machine-learning framework to predict pollutant variability in three tropical Indonesian cities — Makassar, Kendari, and Gorontalo — during 2021 by integrating nonlinear diagnostic screening, multiscale meteorological feature engineering, and Random Forest/XGBoost models. Convergent Cross Mapping (CCM) was used to identify potential nonlinear pollutant–meteorology associations, while Multiple Wavelet Coherence (MWC) was used as an exploratory multivariable coherence-screening tool. These diagnostics were combined with rolling statistics and Discrete Wavelet Transform features for model training and interpretation using SHAP. The proposed models outperformed persistence, ARIMA(1,1,1), and standard machine-learning baselines across all nine city–pollutant combinations. Predictive performance was strongest for PM2.5 and SO2, with R2 values of 0.853 for Makassar PM2.5, 0.805 for Kendari PM2.5, 0.807 for Kendari SO2, and 0.851 for Gorontalo SO2. Sensitivity analyses showed that only rainfall–PM2.5 in Makassar and rainfall–PM10 in Kendari remained robust across original, detrended, and monthly-anomaly CCM tests, indicating that other CCM-derived relationships should be interpreted as exploratory nonlinear associations rather than definitive causal pathways. After red-noise Monte Carlo testing, cone-of-influence exclusion, and false discovery rate correction, MWC-derived coherence patterns did not retain robust statistical significance and were therefore used only for exploratory feature screening. SHAP analysis indicated city-specific model reliance on different predictor groups: PM10 dominated Makassar PM2.5 prediction, gaseous-pollutant and meteorological predictors were important in Gorontalo, and temporally aggregated wind and thermal predictors contributed strongly in Kendari. These findings show that pollutant variability in regional tropical cities is structured by city-specific combinations of inter-pollutant co-variability, rainfall-related particulate removal, ventilation, and low-frequency meteorological persistence. The framework provides an association-based and physically informed approach for improving air-quality prediction and supporting targeted management in developing tropical urban environments.

explanation: 该论文研究南京地区挥发性有机化合物（VOCs）的垂直分布和区域来源贡献，涉及大气成分分析和近地表及低对流层（0-1000米）的观测，与大气环境遥感技术（如空气质量监测和大气成分分析）密切相关。
authors: Yanling Wang;Bin Zhu;Siqi Yang;Wen Lu;Shuangshuang Shi;Zhuyi Jiang;Li Xia;Chenyan Qian;Jinhui Gao
title: The differentiated VOC species vertical profiles and regional source contributions induced by transport and their reactivities over Nanjing
abstract:
•The profiles of two VOC pairs (ethene–ethane, xylene–toluene) were simulated and validated. •Local VOC contributions decreased with altitude, while external contributions increased. •Low-reactivity VOCs (ethane and toluene) affect farther downwind regions than reactive VOCs. 
 High-temporal and spatial resolution vertical observations of volatile organic compounds (VOCs) are scarce compared to near-surface measurements, limiting an in-depth understanding of their vertical photochemical characteristics and spatiotemporal variations. This study integrated autumn lower-tropospheric (0∼1000 m) VOC sounding observations over Nanjing, China, from 1 to 15 November 2020, with the WRF-Chem model to examine the vertical distributions of typical VOC species (ethene, ethane, xylene, and toluene). Results demonstrated the model's effectiveness in capturing surface and vertical VOC and meteorological profiles, along with their key regulating processes. For instance, the diurnal variation of the boundary layer (BL) governed the surface VOC concentrations, characterized by higher values in the morning and evening and lower values in the afternoon, and inversion led to VOCs accumulation near the surface. Furthermore, using the VOCs tagging method coupled into WRF-Chem, it was found that wind field profiles variation dominated the difference in VOCs regional source profiles. Moreover, local source (YRD-NJ: Nanjing) contributed largely to near-surface VOCs of Nanjing (57.0%, 27.5%, 61.4%, and 49.9% for ethene, ethane, xylene, and toluene, respectively) and diminished with altitude (16.1%, 2.6%, 25.3%, and 12.1% at 900 m, respectively), while the contributions from external sources correspondingly increased. Significantly, low-reactivity VOC species had longer lifetimes and thus were more easily affected by farther source contributions at different altitudes (e.g., ethane and toluene, 60% to 90% and 20% to 80% from surface to 900m under northwesterly winds), compared to high-reactivity VOC species (ethene and xylene, 8% to 67% and 3% to 62%).

explanation: 该论文研究了河内的颗粒物中多环芳烃及其氯代衍生物的发生、季节变化、排放源和潜在源区，与大气环境遥感技术中的空气质量监测密切相关。
authors: Dat Nguyen;Quang Tran Vuong;Phan Quang Thang;Jeong-Tae Ju;Takeshi Ohura;Sung-Deuk Choi
title: Polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons and their chlorinated derivatives in atmospheric particles from central Hanoi, Vietnam: Seasonal variation, sources, and regional transport
abstract:
•Particle-bound PAHs and ClPAHs were measured in Hanoi in two seasons. •Both compound groups showed clear seasonality, peaking in the cold season. •Low-molecular-weight PAHs and ClPAHs were more abundant in the cold season. •Fossil fuel combustion, straw burning, and waste incineration were the major sources. •Areas to the northeast and southwest of Hanoi were source regions for both groups. 
 Polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons (PAHs) and their chlorinated derivatives (ClPAHs) are environmental pollutants that pose significant risks to human health. In this study, total suspended particles were collected during cold and warm periods in central Hanoi, Vietnam, to investigate the occurrence, seasonal variations, emission sources, and potential source regions of particle-bound PAHs and ClPAHs. The mean concentrations of Σ21 PAHs and Σ24 ClPAHs were 80.5 ± 53.9 ng/m3 and 6.92 ± 4.54 pg/m3, respectively, over the entire sampling period. Higher concentrations of both PAHs and ClPAHs were observed in the cold period than in the warm period. Phenanthrene, fluoranthene, and pyrene were the most abundant PAHs. The prevalence of 3–4 ring PAHs was likely attributable to local emissions and long-range atmospheric transport (LRAT), possibly facilitated by particle coating processes during transport. Among ClPAHs, 2-chloroanthracene and 9-chlorophenanthrene predominated, which may reflect atmospheric secondary formation and subsequent LRAT. Diagnostic ratios and non-negative matrix factorization suggested that PAHs were mainly associated with traffic emissions and biomass burning, with smaller contributions from coal combustion, waste incineration, and other industrial activities. Significant positive correlations between Σ21 PAH and Σ24 ClPAH concentrations suggested shared emission sources. Potential source contribution function results identified eastern/northeastern and southwestern regions as potential source areas during the cold and warm periods, respectively. These findings highlight that regional transport of PAHs and ClPAHs may influence air quality in neighboring countries and emphasize the need to consider both local and transboundary sources for effective air pollution mitigation.

explanation: 该论文使用机器学习技术重建了泰国的高分辨率XCO2数据，涉及卫星观测（OCO-2）、环境数据（如土地利用、人口密度、气象变量等），并分析了季节性和长期变化，这与大气环境遥感技术（如空气质量监测、卫星观测、大气成分分析）密切相关。
authors: Udomphan Nacksriphod;Korntip Tohsing;Pramet Kaewmesri;Itsara Masiri;Sumaman Buntoung;Somjet Pattarapanitchai
title: Machine-learning reconstruction of high-resolution XCO<sub>2</sub> over Thailand (2015–2024) using integrated satellite and environmental data
abstract:
•Machine learning reconstructed monthly XCO2at 0.05° resolution over Thailand during 2015–2024. •Random Forest outperformed five other models, with R2= 0.979 and RMSE = 1.074 ppm. •Northern Thailand showed higher XCO2associated with biomass burning and land use. •Summer peaks and rainy-season minima reflect seasonal biospheric CO2uptake. •The 10-year record reveals a sustained decadal increase in CO2over Thailand. 
 Reliable assessments of atmospheric carbon dioxide (CO2) over Southeast Asia remain limited due to the sparse spatial coverage of satellite observations, particularly under persistent cloud conditions. This study reconstructs monthly column-averaged CO2 (XCO2) at 0.05° spatial resolution over Thailand for the period 2015–2024 using a machine-learning framework that integrates four categories of predictor variables. (1) Background XCO2 information was derived from Orbiting Carbon Observatory-2 (OCO-2) retrievals and CarbonTracker fields, to represent large-scale atmospheric CO2 variability. (2) Anthropogenic emission proxies, including land-use classifications and population density, were used to characterize the spatial distribution of human-driven CO2 emissions. (3) Biospheric CO2 uptake was represented using MODIS vegetation indices to capture seasonal photosynthetic activity. (4) Meteorological and geographical variables, surface pressure, near-surface temperature, dew-point temperature, wind speed, and terrain elevation, were incorporated to account for atmospheric transport processes and topographic effects. Six machine-learning models were evaluated, with the Random Forest algorithm exhibiting the highest predictive skill when validated against collocated OCO-2 observations (R2 = 0.979; RMSE = 1.074 ppm; bias = −0.032 ppm). The reconstructed XCO2 fields show persistently higher concentrations over northern Thailand and lower values in the eastern region, consistent with seasonal and long-term components identified using seasonal-trend decomposition based on LOESS (STL). Spatial hotspot analysis based on the Getis–Ord Gi∗ statistic reveals a well-defined clustering pattern with pronounced seasonal dependence. A clear annual cycle is observed, with maximum CO2 concentrations during summer, minimum during the rainy season, and a subsequent increase in winter. In addition, the decade-long record indicates a sustained increase in atmospheric CO2 over Thailand.

explanation: 该研究涉及大气汞的地球化学循环，分析了不同气候条件下表面雪中的汞测量数据，探讨了温度、海冰和溴对汞沉积的影响，以及大气汞消耗事件（AMDEs）与汞沉积的关系。这些内容与大气环境遥感技术（如空气质量监测、卫星观测和大气成分分析）密切相关。
authors: Veronica Amoruso;Federico Scoto;Azzurra Spagnesi;Giulia Calzolai;Elena Barbaro;Roberto Salzano;David Cappelletti;Stefano Crocchianti;Andrea Spolaor
title: Investigation of factors controlling the mercury geochemical cycle in Kongsfjorden (Svalbard) under contrasting climatic conditions
abstract:
•Comparison of Hg measurements in surface snow under contrasting climatic conditions. •Bromine deposition in snow increased during the year with extensive sea ice. •Non-sea-salt bromine in snow shows an inverse relation with Hg concentrations. •AMDE frequency and intensity were independent to temperature absolute value. •High radiation limited Hg deposition, suggesting Hg(II) photoreduction in snow. 
 In recent decades, Svalbard's climate has undergone significant changes. However, amidst a nearly constant warming trend, 2020 emerged as an anomaly, marked by unusually low temperatures, a strong polar vortex, and extensive sea ice coverage throughout the winter season. Two sampling campaigns were conducted in the Gruvebadet Snow Research Site (GSRS) in Kongsfjorden (Ny-Ålesund, Svalbard): one during the “cold” 2020 (October 2019 to May 2020) and the other during a “warm” year (October 2018 to May 2019). These campaigns aimed to investigate the potential effects of the distinct climatic conditions on the biogeochemical cycle of mercury (Hg). Mercury, a toxic element, has been extensively studied in polar regions yet uncertainties remain to address changes in the Hg biogeochemical cycle under shifting climatic and atmospheric conditions. By comparing data from the “warm” and “cold” years, this study investigates how factors such as temperature and sea ice modulated Hg deposition patterns. We noted an increase in bromine concentrations in snow during the “cold” year, and in particular in the non-sea-salt component of Br (nssBr and Brenr), which is likely associated to sea ice emissions. However, we do not observe a systematic impact of high bromine on Hg in snow or atmosphere. In both “warm” and “cold” years, Atmospheric Mercury Depletion Events (AMDEs) not followed by Hg deposition on snow are associated with high solar radiation, suggesting possible photo-reduction of Hg in snow. GEM shows good correlation with temperature during all AMDEs, with no distinction between “warm” and “cold” years, suggesting that AMDEs are not directly controlled by absolute value of temperature.

explanation: 该论文研究了-hopanes和-steranes在空气和土壤中的分布和交换，涉及空气颗粒物和气相的监测，以及它们在环境中的行为，这与大气环境遥感技术中的空气质量监测、大气成分分析和气溶胶反演密切相关。
authors: Dominika Bezdeková;Jakub Martiník;Ondrej Letocha;Ludovic Mayer;Petra Přibylová;Gerhard Lammel
title: Hopanes and steranes in air – addressing gas-particle partitioning and air-soil exchange
abstract:
•Seasonal variation of ambient concentration is very strong. •Some hopanes and steranes are mostly gaseous during warm seasons and particle-bound in winter. •The abundance of the hopanes and steranes in topsoil was dominated by atmospheric deposition. •Observed volatilisation of steranes from grassland soil in a rural environment. •Steranes seem to undergo multihopping-enhanced long-range atmospheric transport. 
 Hopanes and steranes are abundant in atmospheric aerosols of polluted environments, as emitted from mostly fossil fuel burning. While in samples of ambient particulate matter they have been widely used as biomarkers, but little is known about their phase partitioning and possible air-surface exchange of the gaseous fraction. While hopanes and steranes are very stable in soils and bioaccumulative, very little is known needed to assess their atmospheric fate and long-range transport potential.Hopanes and steranes in air particulate and gas phases were monitored over 5 years, and in air, air equilibrated with soil and in soil samples during 2 weeks at a rural background site. The air concentrations of 5 targeted hopanes ranged <0.013–1.33 (0.17 ± 0.22, median 0.080) ng m−3 and of 5 targeted steranes <0.002–0.093 (0.015 ± 0.020, median 0.0085) ng m−3 which were higher in winter than in spring and fall and very low in summer. While 17α(H)-22,29,30-trisnorhopane, 17α(H),21β(H)-22S- and 17α(H),21β(H)-22R-homohopane are in the ambient aerosol's particulate phase throughout the year, α,α,α-20R-24R-ethylcholestane, 17α(H)-21β(H)-30-norhopane and 17α(H)-21β(H)-hopane are found completely in the particulate phase in winter, but almost completely in the gas phase in summer. A vertical concentration gradient in a grassland soil suggests that the abundance of the hopanes and steranes in topsoil was dominated by atmospheric deposition. In soil, the mass ratio of hopanes and steranes over PAHs (∑10HOP/∑16PAH) was much higher than in air i.e., 0.23 vs. 0.006. Volatilisation of α,β,β-20R-cholestane, α,α,α-20R-cholestane, α,β,β-20R-24S-methylcholestane, α,β,β-20R-24R-ethylcholestane and α,α,α-20R-24R-ethylcholestane from grassland soil was sporadically observed, while 17α(H)-22,29,30-trisnorhopane and 17α(H),21β(H)-22R-homohopane were deposited. This indicates that sterane concentrations in air might be influenced by a secondary source i.e., air-soil exchange.

explanation: 该论文研究沿海雾中的碳和水同位素特征，涉及大气化学和气溶胶处理过程，与大气环境遥感技术（如空气质量监测、大气成分分析、气溶胶检索）密切相关。
authors: Denise C. Napolitano;Dani Khoury;Alexandra J. Boris;Andrea L. Clements;Jeffrey L. Collett;Pierre Herckes
title: Time-resolved carbon and water isotope signatures of coastal fog on the Southern California Coast
abstract:
•Time-resolved water isotope of dissolved organic carbon (δ13CDOC) in coastal fog water. •δ13CDOCconsistently decreased during individual fog events. •Total organic content varied with liquid water content but not with δ13CDOC. •Fog water δ2H and δ18O tracked meteorological and air mass changes. •Carbon and water isotopes provide constraints on fog scavenging and processing. 
 Fog plays an important role in coastal atmospheric chemistry by scavenging particulate matter (PM) and soluble gases, yet the processes governing their chemical evolution remain incompletely understood. Here, we present time-resolved measurements of stable carbon isotopes of dissolved organic carbon (δ13CDOC) and stable water isotopes (δ2H and δ18O) in fog collected during four fog events at Casitas Pass on the southern California coast. Fog samples were collected at 1–2 h resolution and analyzed together with total organic carbon (TOC), liquid water content (LWC), and size-segregated PM. The δ13CDOC values ranged from −27.1‰ to −24.2‰ and decreased systematically during each fog event by approximately 1‰, independent of changes in TOC concentration or LWC. In contrast, TOC exhibited a nonlinear inverse relationship with LWC, consistent with dilution/concentration during fog development/dissipation. The decoupling of isotopic composition from bulk carbon concentrations indicates that δ13CDOC is influenced by isotope fractionation associated with physical scavenging and in-droplet processing rather than concentration-driven effects alone. Coarse mode PM was generally enriched in stable carbon isotopes of total carbon (δ13CTC) relative to fine particles, suggesting preferential scavenging and deposition of isotopically heavier organic material in coarse particles during fog events. Fog water δ2H and δ18O values defined a distinct cloud water line below the meteoritic water lines, and temporal variability reflected changes in temperature and air mass origin. These results demonstrate that time-resolved carbon and water isotope measurements provide complementary constraints on fog evolution and aerosol processing in coastal environments.

explanation: 该论文研究了废料能源厂排放的挥发性化合物对环境和健康的影响，涉及空气质量和大气成分分析，与大气环境遥感技术中的空气质量监测和大气成分分析密切相关。
authors: Guiying You;Hefan Liu;Xiaoya Dou;Rui Sun;Jing Li;Shaodong Xie
title: Multi-pathway environmental and health impacts of volatile compound emissions from waste-to-energy plants in China
abstract:
•142 volatile species, including 16 sulfur compounds, were quantified from three representative WtE plants. •Sulfides dominated emissions (44-89%) and contributed 80-98% of total odor impact. •OVOCs accounted for 67% of national OFP, while SOAP was driven by OVOCs (42%) and alkanes (41%). •Carcinogenic risks (1.75×10−4-3.61 × 10−3) exceeded regulatory benchmarks, mainly from naphthalene and benzene. •A national-scale, sulfur-inclusive, species-resolved emission inventory (4200 t/a) was established. 
 Municipal solid waste (MSW) incineration has become the dominant waste treatment strategy in China. However, volatile organic compounds (VOCs) and sulphides emitted from waste-to-energy (WtE) plants remain insufficiently characterized. Here, field measurements were conducted at three representatively Chinese WtE plants (142 species). We constructed a national-provincial-city-plant-species level emission inventory for the sector, which is among the first to integrate field-measured source profiles with nationwide facility-level data. Total VOC concentrations ranged from 947 to 3838 μg/m3, with sulfides dominating plant-level composition (44-89%). In the integrated source profile, there are sulfides (52%), OVOCs (21%), and BVOCs (14%). Odor activity values (OAVs) ranged from 1.95 × 103 to 1.74 × 104, with sulfides contributing 80-98% in total. Ozone formation potential (OFP) ranged from 226 to 11547 μg/m3, dominated by OVOCs (21-84%), which contributed 67% of national OFP (6079 t/a). Secondary organic aerosol potential (SOAP) ranged from 0.43 to 4.03 μg/m3 at plant level and 0-13.30 t/a provincially, primarily driven by OVOCs (42%) and alkanes (41%). Incremental lifetime cancer risks (ILCR) were 1.75×10−4-3.61 × 10−3, with naphthalene and benzene accounting for ∼82% of total ILCR. Non-carcinogenic hazard indices (HI) reached up to 7.74, driven by aldehydes. The nationwide VOCs emissions estimated from 1064 WtE facilities were 4200 t/a, with Jiangsu Province contributing 53% in total. Sulfides (2201 t/a), OVOCs (885 t/a), and BVOCs (558 t/a) were the dominant chemical groups. It fills the knowledge gap in detailed VOC characterization and emission quantification from China's WtE sector. It provides a critical insight for future emission control, air quality management, and health risk mitigation.

explanation: 该论文研究长江三角洲地区臭氧污染的成因，涉及大气环境遥感技术中的空气质量监测和数值模拟方法，因此与大气环境遥感技术密切相关。
authors: Duxi Tang;Chenchao Zhan;Yuxi Zheng;Cheng Yuan;Tianliang Zhao
title: Subtropical high and typhoon drive regional ozone pollution in the Yangtze River Delta: Insights from multi-year observations and numerical simulations
abstract:
•Regional O3pollution days in the YRD increased at 3.3 d y−1during 2014–2023. •The SHP, UPP and TCP dominated regional O3pollution in the YRD. •Weak winds and subsidence favored O3accumulation under all three weather types. •The SHP, UPP and TCP corresponded to local, regional and marine transport. 
 The Yangtze River Delta (YRD), one of the most developed regions in China, has experienced severe ozone (O3) pollution in recent years, with regional O3 pollution becoming increasingly prominent. Based on long-term observational data from 2014 to 2023, this study applied the T-PCA weather typing method to identify synoptic weather types associated with regional O3 pollution in the YRD. Numerical simulations were further conducted to investigate the formation mechanisms of O3 pollution under each weather type. The number of regional O3 pollution days in the YRD increased at a rate of 3.3 d y−1 from 2014 to 2023. Three weather types associated with regional O3 pollution were identified: the subtropical high pattern (SHP), the uniform pressure pattern (UPP) and the typhoon circulation pattern (TCP). The SHP accounted for 29.9% of all cases and occurred mainly in April–May, when temperatures were relatively low (23.2 °C) and photochemical production was suppressed, resulting in the lowest O3 concentration (159.0 μg m−3). Under this type, polluted air masses mainly originated within the YRD (76.4%). The UPP was the most frequent type (40.1%) and occurred mainly in June–July, when temperatures were higher (26.4 °C) and regional transport had a stronger influence. The TCP occurred mainly in August–September and was characterized by the highest temperature (26.9 °C), low wind speed (2.3 m s−1) and an O3 concentration of 161.1 μg m−3. Under the TCP, subsidence was weaker and the proportion of marine air masses increased to 62.7%. As these marine air masses passed over highly urbanized coastal areas, they could transport pollutants into the YRD. Poor dispersion conditions, characterized by weak winds (<3 m s−1) and strong subsidence (∼0.05 Pa s−1), were favorable for regional O3 pollution. However, the formation mechanisms differed among weather types, suggesting that O3 control should consider the meteorological conditions and transport pathways specific to each weather type.

explanation: 该论文研究了氨富集地区PM2.5酸度的季节性变化，并利用机器学习模型（如XGBoost）估算氨浓度，这与大气环境遥感技术中的空气质量监测和成分分析密切相关。
authors: Yunfei Wei;Wei Zhang;Jingshen Zhang;Yan Liu;Qixiang Xu;Nan Jiang
title: Seasonal variation of PM<sub>2.5</sub> acidity in ammonia-enriched regions: A machine learning framework for estimating ammonia concentrations
abstract:
•XGBoost demonstrated superior NH3prediction accuracy (R2= 0.96). •PH peaked in winter (5.8) and reached its lowest value in summer (2.7). •Ca2+dominated autumn pH shifts, RH primarily influenced summer pH dynamics. •TNH3exhibited heightened pH sensitivity at lower concentrations. 
 Aerosol acidity (pH) is a critical parameter determining the health, climatic, and ecological impacts of aerosols. Aerosol pH is affected by many factors. The absence of NH3 concentration data in ammonia-rich regions limits pH estimation based solely on particle composition datasets, making it an ongoing research challenge to determine pH without NH3. This study developed an NH3 concentration prediction framework using three machine learning (ML) models: Random Forest (RF), Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost), and Multilayer Perceptron (MLP). Comparative analysis revealed that the XGBoost model exhibited superior predictive performance, achieving an R2 of 0.96 on the full test dataset, with its reliability further validated through interannual and monthly evaluations. The proposed method was applied to predict NH3 concentrations across four seasons in 2023 for ammonia-rich regions. Seasonal pH values were subsequently calculated using the ISORROPIA-II, revealing the highest pH in winter (5.8), followed by spring, and the lowest in summer (2.7). These variations were primarily driven by seasonal fluctuations in key factors including SO42−, total ammonia (TNH3), and temperature. Sensitivity analysis demonstrated that pH changes are more responsive to TNH3 at lower concentrations, suggesting that reducing NH3 emissions is crucial for achieving PM2.5 compliance targets. This approach provides an innovative solution for aerosol pH estimation in ammonia-rich environments and supports the formulation of science-driven emission control strategies.

explanation: 该研究探讨了醛酮与大气二次有机气溶胶（SOA）的粒子相反应，涉及气溶胶化学成分和性质的变化，这与大气环境遥感技术（如空气质量监测和气溶胶检索）密切相关。
authors: Siqi Tang;Yindong Guo;Xiaolei Yang;Zhen Song;Yali Jin;Shuhui Xue;Jianghe Xiong;Defeng Zhao
title: Direct evidence of particle-phase reactions of aldehydes and ketones with atmospheric secondary organic aerosol components
abstract:
•Both aldehydes and ketones can react with SOA components via Baeyer-Villiger oxidation and peroxyhemiacetal/peroxyhemiketal formation in airborne aerosols. •Reaction product analysis and differentiating carboxylic acids formed by aldehydes and esters by ketones provide direct evidence of Baeyer-Villiger oxidation and peroxyhemiacetal/peroxyhemiketal formation. •Reaction rates were synergistically enhanced by ammonium sulfate seed aerosol and moderate-to-high humidity. •The reaction rate constants were estimated for the two aldehyde oxidation reactions to be on the order of 10−6– 10−5L mol−1s−1under AS-seeded conditions. 
 Carbonyl compounds can react with secondary organic aerosol (SOA) components, such as peroxides, in the particle-phase, which may significantly change aerosol chemical composition and properties. However, direct evidence of such reactions is still lacking and reaction products for aldehyde and ketones under atmospheric relevant conditions are unclear. This study investigated particle-phase reactions of aldehydes and ketones with SOA components using α-pinene ozonolysis-derived SOA (AP-SOA) as a representative. Reaction products were measured using an Extractive Electrospray Ionization Time-of-Flight Mass Spectrometer (EESI-ToF-MS). We found both aldehydes and ketones can react with AP-SOA components, likely peroxides, via two Baeyer-Villiger reaction pathways: oxidation (adding one O in molecular formula forming carboxylic acids from aldehydes and esters from ketones) and peroxyhemiacetal/peroxyhemiketal formation. Carboxylic acids and non-acid compounds were differentiated based on sodium-hydrogen exchange analysis in EESI. We thus provide direct evidence for particle-phase Baeyer-Villiger oxidation of aldehydes and ketones with atmospheric SOA components. Both reaction pathways were efficiently promoted by moderate-to-high relative humidity (RH) and the presence of ammonium sulfate (AS) seed aerosol, which enhances reaction rates likely through enhancing diffusion and acid-catalyzed processes. The reaction rate constants were estimated for the two aldehyde oxidation reactions, which were on the order of 10−6 – 10−5 L mol−1 s−1 under AS-seeded conditions. Particle-phase aging via reactions of carbonyl with SOA components (e.g. peroxides) may be an important transformation pathway for SOA, converting common carbonyls into more oxidized and potentially higher-molecular-weight species and thus change its impacts on climate and health.

explanation: 该论文研究了利用TROPOMI卫星数据和MAPST-Net模型对中国地表NO2浓度的时空估计。NO2是一种主要的空气污染物，卫星遥感技术被用于监测其大范围分布，而论文提出的模型则专注于将卫星观测数据转换为地表浓度。这直接涉及大气环境遥感技术和空气质量监测。
authors: Qiaolin Zeng;Hui Zang;Meng Fan;Liangfu Chen;Songyan Zhu
title: Spatiotemporal estimation of daily surface NO<sub>2</sub> concentrations over China from 2019 to 2024 based on TROPOMI data and MAPST-Net model
abstract:
•Propose a point-plane estimation framework. •Construct a spatiotemporal model with temporal dependencies and spatial encoding. •Improve conventional attention mechanisms and convolutional layers. •Analyze the spatiotemporal patterns of surface NO2concentrations across China. 
 Nitrogen dioxide (NO2), a major atmospheric pollutant, poses serious threats to human health and the environment. The concentrations of atmospheric NO2 are jointly influenced by anthropogenic emissions and natural processes. This reflects the combined effects of human activities and atmospheric dynamics. Monitoring stations can provide accurate time-series measurements, but their spatial coverage is limited. Combined with the short atmospheric lifetime of NO2, this limitation hinders a comprehensive characterization of NO2 spatiotemporal distribution. Satellite remote sensing can offer large-scale tropospheric vertical column densities (VCDs) of NO2. However, an effective model is still required to convert these observations into surface concentrations. This study proposed the Multi-Attention Parallel Spatiotemporal Network (MAPST-Net), consisting of three modules: a spatial information encoder, a temporal information encoder, and a feature decoder. The spatial information encoder extracted and optimized multi-scale spatial features. It integrated a multi-level information aggregation module, global-fused coordinate attention, and channel-wise self-attention. The temporal information encoder integrated differential attention with sequence-channel attention to reduce information redundancy and enhance the learning of temporal dynamics. Finally, the feature decoder combined spatiotemporal features using a Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP) to estimate NO2 concentrations. The model achieved satisfactory performance, with sample-based cross-validation R2 of 0.799 and RMSE of 5.722 μg/m3. Accordingly, a nationwide surface NO2 concentration dataset from 2019 to 2024 was generated at a spatial resolution of 0.05°. This dataset provides a technical foundation and high-quality data for accurate NO2 estimation, analyses of spatiotemporal patterns, and the development of air quality management strategies.

explanation: 该论文研究中国1960年至2019年多环芳烃（PAHs）排放的毒性和主要驱动因素，涉及空气污染物排放、来源解析和毒性评估，与大气环境遥感技术中的空气质量监测和成分分析密切相关。
authors: Xiao Yun;Huizhong Shen;Jin Li;Yuanzheng Zhang;Shuxiu Zheng;Rong Dai;Jinghang Wang;Haoran Xu;Wenxiao Zhang;Guofeng Shen;Jianmin Ma;Shu Tao
title: Temporal trends in toxicities and major drivers of polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbon emissions in China from 1960 to 2019
abstract:
•First quantitative analysis of temporal trends of PAH emission toxicities in China. •Quantified major drivers of PAH emission toxicity changes via decomposition analysis. •Switching to clean energy and stove upgrading effectively cut residential toxicities. •Urbanization and switching to coal did not help reduce residential PAH toxicities. •39.2% of total PAH emissions attributed by coal resulted in 61.5% of toxicities. 
 Polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons (PAHs) are a class of crucial air pollutants in China. Composition profiles and toxicities were not further discussed in previous PAH emission inventories, and an accurate quantification of emissions was important for evaluating major driving factors for PAH toxicities in China. In this study, we developed a high-resolution PAH emission inventory in China from 1960 to 2019, with updated emission factors (EFs) in industrial, residential, and transportation sectors. Emissions of priority PAHs totaled 78.7 Gg (51.5 – 134 as the 50% interval) in China in 2019, with industrial (60.6%) and residential (24.1%) sectors dominating total emissions. Total PAH emissions peaked at 1995 and then gradually decreased since the 2000s driven by promotion of air pollution abatement policies. As total PAH emissions decreased, source profiles and composition profiles changed, and PAH toxicities decreased continuously. The decline of PAH emission toxicities was mainly driven by residential energy switching from solid fuels to clean energy, residential stove upgrading, and phasing out beehive coke ovens, while urbanization and residential energy switching from biomass fuels to coal could not effectively reduce PAH emission toxicities.

explanation: 该论文研究的是从FY-3F卫星的HIRAS-II高光谱红外大气探测仪中获取全球氨（NH3）的能力，并评估其性能。这直接涉及大气环境遥感技术，特别是空气质量监测和卫星观测。
authors: Zhili Deng;Yapeng Wang;Minqiang Zhou;Ling Gao;Lin Chen;Xifeng Cao;Lingfeng Zhang;Yuhan Jiang;Haiyang Dou;Xingying Zhang
title: Global ammonia (NH<sub>3</sub>) retrieval from the FY-3F satellite hyperspectral infrared atmospheric sounder and performance assessment
abstract:
•First global NH3retrieval from the FY-3F/HIRAS-II satellite. •HIRAS-II reduces global retrieval RMSE to 0.25%-0.39% (Gen 2). •Retrieval error is suppressed to <3% due to improved SNR. •Results show high consistency with IASI over emission hotspots. 
 Accurate satellite monitoring of atmospheric ammonia (NH3) is critical for understanding the global nitrogen cycle and air quality. However, retrieval precision is often constrained by instrumental noise and surface–atmosphere thermal contrast. This study presents the first comprehensive assessment of global NH3 retrieval capabilities from the second-generation Hyperspectral Infrared Atmospheric Sounder (HIRAS-II) onboard the FY-3F satellite (morning orbit), comparing it with the first-generation FY-3D/HIRAS. By adapting a physical retrieval algorithm to the enhanced sensor characteristics, we quantify the improvements in detection sensitivity and accuracy. Results demonstrate that HIRAS-II achieves a substantial leap in performance, with global spectral fitting residuals (RMSE) reducing from 0.40% to 1.20% (Gen 1) to 0.25%–0.39% (Gen 2). This noise reduction effectively suppresses mean retrieval errors from >4% to less than 3%, significantly improving data stability in high-latitude and background regions. Validation against IASI/Metop-B products reveals high consistency over major emission hotspots, with a mean bias of 7.83 × 1015 molecules/cm2 (corresponding to a relative bias of 0.48%) and a standard deviation of 1.72 × 1016 molecules/cm2. These findings confirm that FY-3F/HIRAS-II provides a high-quality global NH3 dataset that complements afternoon observations, offering reliable support for monitoring diurnal variations and emission fluxes.

explanation: 该论文研究南印度的PM2.5来源解析，涉及空气质量监测、化学成分分析、受体模型和GIS数据等，与大气环境遥感技术密切相关，例如空气质量监测和大气成分分析。
authors: Hema Bhatt;Syed Azharuddin Hashmi;Akila Muthalagu;Vikas Kumar Meena;Asif Qureshi
title: Regional source apportionment of PM<sub>2.5</sub> in South India
abstract:
•First, year-long PM2.5regional source apportionment study in South India. •Frequent exceedance of WHO PM2.5guidelines indicating sustained exposure. •Chemical speciation, receptor modelling and mass-meteorology analysis identified biomass burning and urban dust as dominant sources. •Nearest-neighbour analysis using GIS data, government, and field-based data strengthen the source apportionment study. •Seasonal shifts strongly influenced emission source distribution. 
 Air quality in India is generally a cause for concern. Identifying dominant PM2.5 emission sources through regional source apportionment is essential for designing and supporting effective air quality regulations, yet such studies remain sparse across South India. We present the first year-long (2019) PM2.5 receptor-modelling analysis in Sangareddy, India, as part of the COALESCE (Carbonaceous Aerosol Emissions, Source Apportionment, and Climate Impacts) project, filling a regional gap in South-Indian air quality research. Mean daily PM2.5 concentration (42 ± 40 μg/m3) often exceeded the corresponding WHO air quality guideline (15 μg/m3). Chemical speciation (water-soluble inorganic ions, trace metals, carbonaceous compounds) information, coupled with dispersion-normalized positive matrix factorization (PMF), identified five potential sources. The five-factor solution was statistically validated through Q/Qexp (0.75), bootstrap (BS), DISP, BS-DISP and FPEAK diagnostics, with all factors achieving 95-100% recovery. The resolved sources were biomass burning (rice husk in brick kilns rather than stubble burning, 22 ± 1 μg/m3; 28%), urban dust (24 ± 3 μg/m3; 30%), vehicular emission (10 ± 2 μg/m3; 13%), secondary aerosols (18 ± 1 μg/m3; 22%), and industry (6 ± 0.4 μg/m3; 7%). Seasonal analysis revealed pre-monsoon crustal peaks and post-monsoon/winter anthropogenic dominance. Together, the Conditional Probability Function (CPF), Concentration-Weighted Trajectory (CWT), and pollution rose analysis pinpointed the dominant source direction. Nearest-neighbour analysis, supported by GIS and field data, attributed contribution to specific activity clusters: brick kilns and agricultural fields to the east, industrial and mining zones to the southeast, and the Sangareddy-Hyderabad traffic corridor to the south. This integrated airshed-based approach uniquely enabled both quantification and geographic attribution of PM2.5 sources.

explanation: 该论文研究化学机制对臭氧生成和自由基预算的影响，涉及空气质量建模和排放控制策略，与大气环境遥感技术中的空气质量监测和成分分析密切相关。
authors: Kyoung-Min Kim;Duseong S. Jo;Si-Wan Kim;Alan Fried
title: Radical chemistry and ozone responses under different chemical mechanisms in WRF-Chem: Implications for emission control strategies
abstract:
•Chemical mechanism choice alters radical budgets and O3formation. •RACM shows stronger RO2-driven O3production in urban regions. •T1-MOZART enhances BVOC-driven radical chemistry in rural areas. •O3responses to emission reductions differ substantially between mechanisms. 
 Understanding how chemical mechanism formulations influence radical chemistry and O3 formation is critical for accurate air quality modeling and emission control strategies, particularly in chemically complex regions such as East Asia. In this study, we compare the Regional Atmospheric Chemistry Mechanism (RACM) and the Model for Ozone and Related Chemical Tracers (T1-MOZART) within the WRF-Chem framework over the Seoul Metropolitan Area (SMA) and South Korea during the KORUS-AQ campaign period to examine their impacts on radical budgets and O3 production. RACM simulated higher surface O3 than T1-MOZART (MDA8 O3: 64.2 vs. 58.1 ppb over the SMA), driven by stronger HO2+NO and RO2+NO reaction rates. Over the SMA, OH and HO2 were 24–59% higher in RACM, and RO2 was more than three times larger, largely due to enhanced anthropogenic and NO3-alkene adduct peroxy radical chemistry. Over South Korea, averaged RO2 was comparable between mechanisms, as enhanced biogenic RO2 in T1-MOZART largely compensated for its weaker anthropogenic radical production. Sensitivity simulations revealed strong nonlinear O3 responses to emission reductions. A 30% NOx reduction increased MDA8 O3 over the SMA by 6.9% in RACM and 8.7% in T1-MOZART, whereas O3 decreased over South Korea, reflecting contrasting urban and regional production regimes. A 30% VOC reduction led to larger O3 decreases in RACM (−7.1%) than in T1-MOZART (−5.9%), reflecting stronger dependence on anthropogenic VOC-derived RO2 in RACM. These findings demonstrate that RO2 formation pathway differences significantly influence simulated O3 responses to emission controls, highlighting the importance of accurately representing VOC-derived radical chemistry.

explanation: 该论文研究了温带海草物种 *Zostera marina* 的生物成因挥发性有机化合物 (BVOC) 排放通量，这与大气环境遥感技术中的空气质量监测和大气成分分析密切相关。研究使用了围栏室和 PTR-ToF-MS 技术来检测和测量 BVOC，这些技术和测量对象（如 BVOC 排放）都与大气环境遥感技术相关。
topic_words: ['Biogenic Volatile Organic Compound', 'Zostera marina', 'BVOC fluxes', 'air quality monitoring', 'atmospheric composition analysis']
authors: A. Saunier;J. Kammer;M. Rocco;S. Coquin;E. Ormeno;H. Wortham;C. Fernandez
title: Evaluation of Biogenic Volatile Organic Compound fluxes from a temperate seagrass species, Zostera marina
abstract:
•BVOC fluxes fromZ. marinawere followed over four days of measurements. •Six major compounds were detected with different diurnal cycles. •Both emissions and uptakes were observed but globally,Z. marinabeds could act as a BVOC sink rather than a BVOC source. •Nighttime emissions were also detected, suggesting that marine BVOCs are not only linked to light. 
 Over the past decade, marine Biogenic Volatile Organic Compound (BVOC) fluxes have received increasing attention because of their importance for global biogeochemical cycles. However, most available data have been gathered from pelagic areas, leaving benthic environments — particularly seagrass meadows — remain understudied. Characterizing BVOC fluxes from these ecosystems is therefore essential. In this study, we investigated BVOC fluxes from Zostera marina, under controlled laboratory conditions, using enclosure chambers coupled to a PTR-ToF-MS over diurnal cycles. Six major compounds were tentatively assigned to: methanol (m/z 33.03), acetonitrile (m/z 42.04), acetaldehyde (m/z 45.03), acetone (m/z 59.05), DMS (m/z 63.03), and C5H8 (m/z 69.07, probably attributed to isoprene). Most compounds exhibited uptakes while emission rates detected were relatively low, around a few ng gDW-1 h−1. Furthermore, our results also suggest that light could not be the only factor controlling BVOC fluxes, as the highest emissions of methanol or acetone occurred at night, for instance. Due to high uptakes observed in our study, Zostera marina beds may act more as BVOC sinks rather than BVOC sources. Further studies are needed to confirm this result by including more individuals (under laboratory and natural conditions) but also using a wide range of environmental conditions driving both emissions and uptakes. This is necessary to modeling studies at regional and global scale.

explanation: 该论文研究的是大气中氢氯（HCl）的分布和热力学分配，特别是在沿海大气环境中的氯预算和气溶胶酸度，这与大气环境遥感技术中的空气质量监测和大气成分分析密切相关。
authors: Hengqing Shen;Weihang Sun;Xiaorui Chen;Yifan Jiang;Steven Poon;Tao Wang
title: HCl in the coastal atmosphere: chlorine reservoir and thermodynamic partitioning
abstract:
•HCl dominates the coastal chlorine budget (∼50% on average, >70% during daytime). •Thermodynamic models accurately reproduce observed gaseous HCl concentrations. •Gas-phase HCl and HNO3negligibly affect aerosol pH prediction. 
 Atmospheric hydrogen chloride (HCl) is an important tropospheric chlorine reservoir that influences aerosol acidity, halogen cycling, and atmospheric oxidation capacity, yet its partitioning and abundance relative to other chlorine species remain poorly constrained because concurrent multispecies measurements are scarce. Here, we analyze simultaneous measurements of gaseous HCl, Cl2, ClNO2, and particulate Cl− in PM2.5 at a coastal site in Hong Kong to characterize chlorine speciation and evaluate the thermodynamic partitioning of HCl. HCl dominates the measured chlorine budget, contributing ∼50% on average and more than 70% during daytime. ISORROPIA II and E-AIM reproduced the observed gaseous HCl concentrations in forward mode, indicating that the hourly HCl partitioning was broadly consistent with thermodynamic equilibrium under the conditions of this campaign. Across both models, excluding HCl or HNO3 caused only minor changes in predicted aerosol pH, whereas excluding NH3 decreased pH by ∼0.6 units. Complementary XGBoost-SHAP analyses of thermodynamic-model-derived pH consistently identified meteorological conditions, sulfate, and gaseous NH3 among the leading predictors, while HCl and HNO3 made relatively small contributions. Overall, this study establishes HCl as the dominant atmospheric chlorine reservoir in Hong Kong coastal environments, confirms the robustness of thermodynamic equilibrium models for predicting gaseous HCl and pH, and provides an observational benchmark for chlorine chemistry and aerosol acidity in the marine atmosphere.

explanation: 该论文研究了利用GEMS卫星观测数据、GEOS-CF模型模拟数据以及机器学习技术来获取中国24小时无缝近地表NO2浓度。这直接涉及大气环境遥感技术，特别是空气质量监测和卫星观测，因此与题目要求的相关性很高。
authors: Zuowen Zhong;Tongwen Li;Jingan Wu;Yuan Wang;Xuefei Hu
title: Deriving 24-h seamless near-surface NO<sub>2</sub> in China through integration of GEMS observations, model simulations, and machine learning
abstract:
•24-h spatiotemporally continuous near-surface NO2over China is estimated. •GEMS satellite data and GEOS-CF simulation data are integrated for 24-h modeling. •Near-surface hourly NO2estimates show good performance (cross-validation R2> 0.75). •Day-night NO2variations and NO2changes during heavy rainfall events are examined. 
 Near-surface nitrogen dioxide (NO2) is a major air pollutant, and satellite remote sensing enables its large-scale monitoring. Existing estimates from polar-orbiting satellites are generally limited to daily resolution, restricting the understanding of diurnal variability. The new-generation geostationary satellite GEMS allows hourly NO2 retrievals, but spatial gaps and missing nighttime data remain challenges. Here, we integrate hourly GEMS observations with 24-h continuous GEOS-CF simulation data to generate seamless 24-h near-surface NO2 estimates across China. A reconstruction and residual-correction framework is first applied to fill the missing GEMS tropospheric NO2 column density (TCDNO2). Based on the reconstructed TCDNO2, near-surface NO2 concentrations are subsequently estimated. Validation against ground sites shows improved agreement for reconstructed TCDNO2 (RMSE = 1.008 × 1016 molecules/cm2) compared with GEOS-CF data (RMSE = 2.699 × 1016 molecules/cm2). The near-surface NO2 estimation model also shows strong performance, with sample-based and temporal-based cross-validation R2 values of 0.76 and 0.76, and MAE (RMSE) of 9.08 μg/m3 (13.35 μg/m3) and 9.12 μg/m3 (13.35 μg/m3), respectively. The near-surface NO2 estimates in China in 2023 show distinct spatial patterns and evident seasonal variability, with lower levels in spring and summer and higher levels in autumn and winter. The NO2 estimates also effectively capture the impact of heavy rainfall events in the Beijing–Tianjin–Hebei region during 28 July–4 August 2023.

explanation: 该论文研究了印度西部城市站点在冬季和夏季的空气环境异戊二烯来源，并分析了COVID-19大流行期间封锁措施的影响。研究涉及对异戊二烯等污染物的连续测量，以及温度、人类活动和生物来源对空气质量的影响，这与大气环境遥感技术（如空气质量监测）密切相关。
authors: L.K. Sahu;Mansi Gupta;Nidhi Tripathi;Dibyani Singh;Ravi Yadav;T.K. Sunil Kumar
title: Sources of ambient air isoprene during winter and summer seasons and impact of COVID-19 pandemic lockdown at an urban site in western India
abstract:
•Distinct diurnal patterns of isoprene in urban air with higher nighttime concentrations in winter and daytime concentrations in summer. •An exponential increase in biogenic isoprene concentration with temperature led to a significant increasing trend from winter to summer. •Strong temperature-dependent biogenic VOC emissions could have important implications for urban air quality in summer. 
 Time-resolved continuous measurements of ambient isoprene mixing ratios at an urban location in western India were conducted from January to May 2020. The measurement period represents the gradual changes in meteorological parameters from winter to summer, as well as the reductions in anthropogenic emissions from the pre-lockdown phase of COVID-19 to the lockdown period. The day-to-day variations between 0.78 and 3.25 ppb during January-March and 1.07-2.25 ppb during April-May were associated mainly with the variabilities in night and day data, respectively. Diurnal patterns with higher evening-early morning and daytime concentrations in winter and summer months resemble the features of predominant anthropogenic and biogenic emissions, respectively. The analysis of the ratios of isoprene to aromatic compounds revealed the influence of biogenic sources on diurnal and seasonal variations. The afternoon isoprene/aromatic ratios increased exponentially at higher temperatures (25-42°C), leading to increasing trends of biogenic contribution during the winter-to-summer transition period. Despite predominant biogenic contributions, reductions in anthropogenic emissions due to the COVID-19 lockdowns could also be a factor for large enhancements of isoprene/xylenes (23.0-30.5 ppb ppb−1), isoprene/ethylbenzene (28.7-37.2 ppb ppb−1), and isoprene/benzene (5.1-9.6 ppb ppb−1) ratios than in winter. The present study shows that there are no significant differences in isoprene mixing ratios between winter and summer seasons. However, tracer-based analysis shows a significant seasonality in the relative apportionment between anthropogenic and biogenic contributions. In addition to relative changes in anthropogenic and biogenic contributions, the trend of the isoprene mixing ratio also reflects the impact of meteorological factors influencing photo-oxidation and dilution.

explanation: 该论文研究了干旱对德克萨斯州PM2.5浓度的影响，涉及空气质量监测和干旱对大气成分的影响，与大气环境遥感技术（如空气质量监测）密切相关。
authors: Kazi Fahad Quddus;Yuxuan Wang;Geoffrey Roberts
title: Using surface observations to quantify the effects of droughts on PM<sub>2.5</sub> in Texas during 2005 – 2024
abstract:
•20-year analysis reveals PM2.5typically increases during summer/fall in Texas. •Drought sharply increases winter PM2.5but reduces it in summer. •Soil dust is the most consistently drought-amplified component. 
 Fine particulate matter (PM2.5) is a major air quality and public health concern, yet its response to drought remains insufficiently understood at regional scales. Using 20 years of observations from 52 monitoring sites across Texas (2005–2024) combined with weekly United States Drought Monitor classifications, we quantify how drought alters both PM2.5 mass and chemical composition. Severe drought increases PM2.5 during winter and spring (+14% and +13.5%, respectively) but frequently reduces concentrations in summer and fall (−8.6% and −4.8%). Speciation analysis shows that soil dust is the most consistently drought-enhanced component, while carbonaceous aerosols exhibit seasonally variable responses, with organic carbon showing increases at some sites in winter and decreases during summer drought conditions. Secondary inorganic aerosols (sulfate and nitrate) show spatially heterogeneous responses across sites. These results indicate that drought–PM2.5 interactions in Texas are strongly season-dependent and are associated with the combined influence of dust emissions, carbonaceous aerosol variability, and secondary aerosol processes. Understanding these seasonal and spatial responses is essential for air quality management in Texas and other drought-prone regions.

explanation: 该论文研究了二次气溶胶形成对臭氧生成的影响，涉及气溶胶光学厚度、光解率等与大气环境遥感技术相关的内容，因此与主题密切相关。
authors: Gang Zhao;Jinyuan Yu;Song Guo;Min Hu;Linghan Zeng;Weili Lin;Chunsheng Zhao
title: Secondary aerosol formation Would Promote the Formation of Ozone: Evidence from a Yangtze River Delta campaign
abstract:
•The column photolysis rate within the boundary layer increases with the increment of scattering aerosol optical depth. •Secondary aerosol formation would promote the photolysis rate within the boundary layer and the formation of ozone. •Positive feedback between secondary aerosol formation and photolysis rate leads to the synthetic pollution of aerosols and ozone. 
 In recent years, China has frequently experienced synergistic pollution events involving fine particulate matter (PM2.5) and ozone (O3). This study investigates the impact of secondary aerosol formation on O3 generation. Based on the NCAR Tropospheric Ultraviolet and Visible radiation model, we found that the integrated photolysis rates within the boundary layer are inversely proportional to absorbing aerosol optical depth but directly proportional to scattering aerosol optical depth. This implies that the secondary formation of non-light-absorbing aerosols can enhance the photolysis rates within the boundary layer, thereby promoting O3 generation, which further promotes the atmospheric oxidation capacity and thus enhances the secondary aerosol formation. The positive feedback of PM2.5 and O3 through aerosol photolysis rates would further lead to the synergistic pollution of PM2.5 and O3. Field measurements at the Yangtze River Delta in China showed a positive relationship between O3 concentrations and the ratio of secondary aerosol to black carbon mass concentration, which agrees well with our model study. This study provides a new insight into the synergistic pollution of PM2.5 and O3 in Eastern China.

explanation: 该论文研究的是西北喜马拉雅地区气溶胶的氧化应激及其化学驱动因素，涉及空气质量监测和大气成分分析，与大气环境遥感技术密切相关。
authors: Aditi Rana;Preyas Prabhakaran;Rajdeep Singh;Harish C. Phuleria;Sayantan Sarkar
title: Sources and chemical drivers of aerosol oxidative stress in surrogate lung fluid: insights from the Northwestern Himalayas
abstract:
•OPvand •OHvshowed distinct seasonal patterns in the northwestern Himalayas •OPvwas associated with aged organics while •OHvwas driven by transition metals •RF-MLR identified Mn, Fe, Cu, Co, EC, and OC as drivers of oxidative stress •Industrial, secondary, and coal combustion sources majorly constrained OPv and •OHv 
 Fine-mode aerosols induce oxidative stress via generation of reactive oxygen species (ROS), which is considered a key mechanism underlying the adverse health effects of air pollution. While the acellular dithiothreitol (DTT) assay is a widely used to assess the oxidative potential (OP), it may not fully capture PM2.5-induced oxidative stress, and complementary measurements of hydroxyl radical (•OH) generation are needed. In the Indian context, the sources and chemical drivers of aerosol-induced OP and •OH remain poorly constrained over erstwhile pristine receptor locations such as the Himalayas. To address this gap, this work quantifies PM2.5-induced oxidative stress in surrogate lung fluid in the northwestern Himalayan midlands using the DTT and •OH assays, along with measurements of bioaccessible metals, ionic species and carbonaceous fractions. This is followed by the application of machine learning (ML) and receptor modeling frameworks to identify key chemical drivers and to apportion source contributions to oxidative stress. The annual mean volume-normalized DTT (OPv) and •OH generation activity (•OHv) were 0.30 ± 0.15 nmol min−1 m−3 and 0.02 ± 0.007 nmol min−1 m−3, respectively. OPv and •OHv showed a contrasting temporal trend, with highest levels in the winter for the former (0.35 ± 0.17 nmol min−1 m−3) and in the summer for the latter (0.019 ± 0.004 nmol min−1 m−3). OPv was clearly enhanced for aged samples dominated by secondary organic aerosol from a mixture of biomass burning (BB) and fossil fuel (FF) sources. Random forest analysis coupled with regression identified FF (with EC and NO3− as tracers) and BB emissions (with OC and K+ as tracers), and transition metals (Mn, Fe, Cu, Co; potentially of industrial origin) as the major predictors of oxidative stress. The receptor model positive matrix factorization (PMF) identified 7 sources of ambient aerosol overall, out of which, industrial emissions and secondary formation were responsible for 83% of OPv while regional coal combustion accounted for 75% of •OHv. Source-sector analysis highlighted the importance of considering both local/regional and long-range transport. These findings emphasize the need for source as well as chemical-targeted interventions to mitigate health risks in the Himalayan region.

explanation: 该论文研究了首尔市高PM2.5事件分类和污染特征分析，涉及空气质量监测、机器学习分类模型以及PM2.5相关技术，与大气环境遥感技术密切相关。
authors: Sang-Jin Lee;Jeong-Tae Ju;Jong Hyuk Choi;Sung Tae Kim;ChanJung An;Hae-Jin Jung;Sung-Deuk Choi
title: Development of a machine learning-based classification model for high PM<sub>2.5</sub> events and pollution characterization in Seoul, South Korea
abstract:
•High PM2.5events in Seoul (2016–2023) were classified into four distinct cases. •Pollution characteristics were analyzed with PMF, CWT, CAMx, and meteorological data. •Each case was distinguished by source types, regions, and combustion processes. •A real-time system was developed to classify and characterize high PM2.5events. •An AI-based PM2.5classification system can support long-term air quality management. 
 Northeast Asia frequently experiences high PM2.5 episodes, and severe pollution in megacities such as Seoul remains a major environmental concern. These episodes arise from multiple emission sources, secondary atmospheric processes, and meteorological conditions. In this study, high PM2.5 events in Seoul from 2016 to 2023 were classified and analyzed, resulting in four distinct pollution cases identified using k-means clustering. PM2.5-related variables, including criteria air pollutants (CAPs), meteorological parameters, and source contributions derived from positive matrix factorization (PMF), were used as input features for clustering. Over the study period, the four cases occurred on 122, 220, 55, and 128 days, respectively, and exhibited clearly different meteorological conditions and source contribution patterns. The four cases broadly represented sulfate-dominated industrial pollution in the warm season, nitrate-dominated combustion pollution in the cold season, severe PM2.5 episodes driven by long-range atmospheric transport from China, and spring pollution under stagnation meteorological conditions involving yellow dust and natural sources. Each case was further examined to quantify contributions from major combustion processes, source types, and source regions using multiple approaches, including PMF, the CAMx chemical transport model, and the concentration-weighted trajectory (CWT) model. Variations in source contributions reflected differing combinations of local emissions, secondary formation, and long-range transport. In addition, a real-time random forest classification model was developed to identify high PM2.5 cases based solely on CAPs and meteorological variables. This framework enables rapid identification of pollution events and provides practical insights for real-time air quality assessment, short-term response planning, and long-term strategies for reducing PM2.5 concentrations.

explanation: 该论文研究了从家用燃气灶中测定的NO2排放率，涉及室内空气监测和污染物分析，这与大气环境遥感技术（如空气质量监测）密切相关。
authors: Xinchen Le;Jianwei Gu;Xu Du;Qiaoqiao Wang;Xiaoliang Qin;Zhi Ning;Ming Zhou;Guiying Li;Taicheng An
title: Determination of NO<sub>2</sub> emission rates from residential gas stoves with continuous indoor monitoring
abstract:
•NO2emission rates from gas stoves measured in 22 households in Guangzhou, China. •Steady-state indoor NO2reach 1107 and 796 μg/m3during use of NG and LPG stoves. •Mean NO2emission rates were 54.3 mg/h (NG stoves) and 40.0 mg/h (LPG stoves). •Indoor NO2shows rapid total decay (mean of 6.5 h−1) due to air exchange and net loss rate. •Neglecting net decay rate in mass-balance model underestimates emission rate by 16-87%. 
 Natural gas (NG) and liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) are often promoted as “clean” residential fuels, yet their combustion emits nitrogen dioxide (NO2), a pollutant with well-documented health risks. Household gas stoves are a predominant source of indoor NO2. We conducted a campaign with on-site monitoring of NO2 emissions in 22 residential homes in Guangzhou, China. The air exchange rates (AER) and NO2 decay rates were measured in each of the households. NO2 concentrations surged rapidly after ignition, reaching steady-state concentrations of 1107.1 μg/m3 (NG) and 796.0 μg/m3 (LPG) within 15∼20 min—far exceeding the World Health Organization's 1-h NO2 guideline (200 μg/m3). Higher gas flow rates or thermal loads are correlated with increased NO2 emission rates. Specifically, for NG with flow rates varying from 0.17 to 0.54 m3/h, the NO2 emission factors increased from 1.36 to 8.32 ng NO2/J. For LPG combustion with flow rates ranging from 12.11 to 69.99 g/h, the NO2 emission factors spanned from 0.30 to 2.28 mg NO2/g LPG. Overall, NG stoves have a higher mean NO2 emission rate (54.3 mg/h) than LPG stoves (40.0 mg/h). Critically, emission rates with models using measured NO2 decay rates were 116-187% that of those using AER alone. It demonstrates the necessity of measuring the NO2 decay rates for accurate NO2 emission quantification for gas stoves. We recommend that future research integrates NO2 decay rate measurements to enhance indoor air quality evaluation and better characterize indoor NOx chemical processes.

explanation: 该论文研究氨的干沉降，这属于大气环境污染物沉降的范畴，与空气质量监测和大气成分分析直接相关。研究中使用了地面观测网络和模型进行模拟，涉及大气环境遥感技术中的地面观测和模型应用。
authors: Colleen B. Baublitz;John T. Walker;Zhiyong Wu;Jesse O. Bash;Michael D. Bell;Da Pan
title: Inferential modeling of dry deposition across the Ammonia Monitoring Network (AMoN)
abstract:
•Modeled field-scale ammonia dry deposition at AMoN sites for 2018. •Tested the sensitivity of ammonia dry deposition to model inputs. •By area, deciduous broadleaf forests are a disproportionate sink for ammonia. •Soil pH, wind andαcutdominate the ammonia depositional sensitivity. •Pairing passive ammonia and micrometeorology measurements could improve constraints. 
 Ammonia dry deposition is a growing contributor to the U.S. nitrogen budget, but there are not currently routine measurements of this process. This lack of observational constraints hinders model evaluation relevant for projections of air quality and ecosystem impacts. We use concentration measurements from the Ammonia Monitoring Network (AMoN) and the field-scale version of the Surface Tiled Aerosol and Gaseous Exchange (STAGE) model to estimate ammonia dry deposition across AMoN sites in 2018. We find that the largest ammonia fluxes (including net deposition or re-emission) occur in the US midwest on an annual basis and extend eastward in the summer. Among landcover categories sampled within a 3 km radius of each AMoN site, deciduous and mixed forests contribute most to the total annual deposition flux via the cuticular pathway. With a series of sensitivity simulations, we find that our results are sensitive to the parameter αcut, which reflects the role of relative humidity in cuticular exchange, as well as to soil pH and soil sorption parameters, especially at high soil pH. The empirically derived αcut is relatively uncertain, while soil pH and sorption parameters are often assumed to be invariant or are unspecified in chemical transport models. Targeted measurements that improve our understanding of the role of relative humidity and related soil dynamics in ammonia concentration variability—for example, over alkaline soils in the western USA—could improve model representation of ammonia dry deposition.

explanation: 该论文研究了夏季热浪期间PM2.5的形成、成分和来源解析，涉及大气环境中的空气质量监测和二次污染物的形成机制，与大气环境遥感技术中的空气质量监测和成分分析密切相关。
authors: Guoxian Zhang;Mengdi Song;Chunli Feng;Qianqian Liu;Haoqiang Cheng;Shiqiang Sun;Zhi Li;Chen Zhu;Leyan Wang;Zhaowu Zhou;Fante Xu
title: Formation, composition and source apportionment of PM<sub>2.5</sub> during a summer heatwave: a case study
abstract:
•Heatwaves altered PM2.5composition by promoting sulfate and SOA formation while suppressing nitrate via ammonium nitrate thermal decomposition. •A RH threshold of ∼35% controlled nitrate formation, making SNA the dominant driver of PM2.5pollution after the heatwave. •Source apportionment revealed PM2.5was mainly from secondary and combustion sources, with vehicle and coal-related emissions as key primary sources. 
 Fine particulate matter (PM2.5) poses serious threats to human health and atmospheric environment, yet the mechanisms by which extreme heatwaves influence PM2.5 chemical composition and secondary formation remain unclear. In this study, online measurement was conducted during a typical summer heatwave in Xuzhou, Jiangsu Province, to explore the dynamic evolution of PM2.5 pollution across Pre-, In-, and Post-heatwave stages. Compared with the pre-HW period, the heatwave increased the daily average PM2.5 concentration by approximately 52%, accompanied by a pronounced restructuring of chemical components. Intense photochemical oxidation promoted the formation of secondary sulfate and organic aerosols, whereas high temperatures suppressed particulate nitrate through thermal decomposition, counteracting the enhancement from elevated oxidation. After the heatwave, declining temperature and relative humidity above ∼35% alleviated the thermodynamic inhibition of nitrate. Accumulated precursors underwent efficient heterogeneous conversion, elevating the SNA proportion to 56.6% and directly triggering the PM2.5 rebound. Source apportionment showed that secondary sources (32.1%) and combustion sources (28.1%) were the dominant contributors to PM2.5, with vehicle emissions and coal-related industrial as the core primary sources. During heatwaves, the effect of NOx reduction on PM2.5 mitigation was significantly weakened by nitrate thermodynamic suppression. After the heatwave, strict control of NOx emissions and strengthened regulation of coal-related emissions are urgently needed to prevent nitrate-driven pollution.

explanation: 该论文研究了颗粒物尺寸和组成对Plantower PMS5003传感器报告的PM2.5浓度的影响，涉及了相对湿度、细颗粒物分布和粗颗粒物质量等与空气质量监测和气溶胶遥感技术相关的关键因素。因此，它与大气环境遥感技术密切相关。
authors: William C. Malm;Bret A. Schichtel;Anthony J. Prenni;Russell Perkins;Amy Sullivan;Mark Tigges
title: The effect of particle size and composition on plantower reported PM2.5 concentrations
abstract:
•64%–80% of PM2.5cf1/PM2.5 variability stems from dry fine-mode size changes. •RH with hygroscopic mass, and coarse mass, each add 10–18% to PM2.5cf1/PM2.5 variability. •RH plus hygroscopic mass increases PM2.5cf1, while more coarse mass suppresses it. •80% of the 1 h PM2.5cf1 observation lie roughly between half and twice the true value. •95% CI for mean predicted PM2.5 from PM2.5cf1 is ±0.06 μg/m3. 
 Since its introduction in 2016, the PurpleAir (PA) monitor, fitted with two Plantower PMS5003 sensors (PMS), has become one of the most widely deployed low-cost aerosol monitoring systems. The National Park Service (NPS) has implemented a PA network to track wildfire smoke and may find them useful for assessing long-term spatial and temporal trends in haze. Uncertainties in PMS derived PM2.5 attributable to relative humidity (RH), presumably in combination with hygroscopic particle growth, have been investigated by others. However, shifts in fine particle size due to secondary particle formation and dust events have not been systematically examined. To better understand these influences, the NPS conducted a field study in Fort Collins, Colorado, deploying particle sizing instruments, semi-continuous inorganic ion analyzers, and several PA sensors. Measurements were obtained at both ambient RH and RH < 40%. Although PMS sensors demonstrated ∼10% precision, expressed as the coefficient of variation between PMS sensors the error term, ET = (PMS PM2.5/reference PM2.5), showed pronounced variability, ranging from about 0.5 to values exceeding 2. Most of the ET variability was associated with shifts in the dry fine-mode volume particle size distribution, which typically peaks near the PMS 0.3 μm detection threshold. These shifts in fine particle size distribution were likely driven by secondary particle formation processes. On the average both RH-dependent particle growth and changes in the coarse mass fraction affected ET with RH tending to elevate ET, whereas higher coarse mass fractions tended to lower it.

explanation: 该论文研究了大气气溶胶的散射增强和辐射效应，涉及气溶胶类型（如粉尘、海洋、棕色碳和黑碳）的识别及其吸湿性，以及气溶胶光学特性（AOPs）在遥感估算和辐射传输模型评估中的应用。这与大气环境遥感技术密切相关，特别是空气质量和大气成分分析领域。
authors: M.M. Jiménez-Martín;M. Sorribas;M. Yela
title: Insights into aerosol scattering enhancement and radiative effects from an intensive wintertime campaign at El Arenosillo, southwestern Europe
abstract:
•Dry AOPs typing resolves simultaneously contrasting hygroscopic behaviors. •Marine, dust, and carbonaceous aerosols exhibit distinct hygroscopic responses. •BC- and BrC-dominated aerosols exhibit deliquescent behaviour at RH ≥ 75%. •Carbonaceous aerosols exhibit variable hygroscopicity linked to aerosol processing. •For BC- and BrC- aerosols, RFE(RH = 85%) decreases up to 15% regarding RH ≤ 40%. 
 Atmospheric aerosol hygroscopicity plays a key role in aerosol-radiation interactions, but its dependence on aerosol type remains poorly constrained, particularly at locations influenced by complex aerosol mixtures. A multiparameter approach which combines the scattering enhancement factor f(λ,RH), the hygroscopic growth parameter γ, the hysteresis index η and humidity-dependent radiative properties in terms of the intensive aerosol optical properties (AOPs) was applied to characterize aerosol-water interactions of different aerosol-dominated regimes during a four-month intensive winter campaign at the coastal site of El Arenosillo (southwestern Europe). Instead of relying on source-region assumptions, aerosol types were identified using exclusively intensive optical properties through an absorption versus scattering Ångström exponents (AAE vs. SAE) classification scheme, whose robustness was evaluated by a Monte Carlo sensitivity analysis. Four dominant aerosol regimes were identified: dust, remote marine, brown carbon (BrC)- and black carbon (BC)- dominated. In terms of hygroscopicity, a strong aerosol-type dependence was observed. Into this framework, f(λ = 550 nm, RH = 85%) mean values ranged from 1.00 ± 0.00 for hydrophobic dust particles to 2.36 ± 0.32 for highly hygroscopic remote marine particles, with 1.56 ± 0.29 and 1.64 ± 0.21 for intermediate but significantly hygroscopic BrC- and BC-dominated aerosols, respectively. Marine aerosols showed deliquescent behaviour around RH ∼60%, while carbonaceous aerosols presented deliquescent transitions around 65% and 74% on BrC- and BC-dominated regimes, respectively. The effect of water uptake on aerosol radiative properties was assessed through RH-dependent changes in single scattering albedo (SSA) and radiative forcing efficiency (RFE). While dust and marine aerosols showed minor SSA(RH) and RFE(RH) sensitivity, carbonaceous aerosols exhibited up to a 15% reduction in RFE(RH = 85%) regarding dry conditions, indicating an enhanced cooling effect under hydration conditions. These results demonstrate that AOPs-based aerosol typing provides an effective framework for retrieving aerosol-dependent hygroscopic parameters which improves the representation of humidity effects in remote-sensing estimations and radiative-transfer modelling assessments.

title: Roots navigate around decay regions by sensing local pH gradients
abstract: Plant tropisms enable roots to navigate complex soils by responding to directional environmental cues. Biological decay, although central to nutrient cycling, also creates microbially active and potentially hostile niches. In this work, we identified “saprotropism,” a previously unrecognized growth response that enables roots to actively bend away from decaying plant-derived matter. Fungal-driven microbial decomposition released organic acids and formed stable pH gradients in surrounding soil, allowing roots to pinpoint decay without direct contact. Root epidermal cells sensed this acidic gradient through the root meristem growth factor peptide-receptor module, converting external pH asymmetry into asymmetric abscisic acid (ABA) distribution. ABA asymmetry drove microtubule reorganization, which was decoded into decay-avoidant root bending. Together, these findings establish microbial decay–derived chemical gradients as an instructive signal for root navigation and expand the framework of microbe-soil-plant communication.

title: The origin, history, and resistance architecture of an invasive urban malaria mosquito in Africa
abstract: The invasive urban malaria vectorAnopheles stephensithreatens 126 million city dwellers in Africa. ControllingAn. stephensirequires greater understanding of its origin, invasion dynamics, and insecticide resistance mechanisms. Analysis of 645 whole genomes sampled across Africa, the Middle East, and Asia supports an invasion scenario in which an initial South Asian introduction established a bridgehead population in Djibouti, which seeded distinct invasion fronts in Sudan, Ethiopia/Kenya, and Yemen. These incursions show contrasting rates and routes of spread shaped by landscape topology. Insecticide resistance is predominantly mediated by metabolic detoxification genes, with resistance haplotypes and copy-number amplifications introduced from South Asia. These findings, alongside a companion genomic resource, enable genomic surveillance ofAn. stephensispread and resistance to aid control strategies.

title: Neural circuits for valence updating in social memory
abstract: Social animals recognize familiar conspecifics and selectively avoid harmful ones. As social relationships shift, continuous updating of social valence is essential, yet the underlying neural mechanisms remain unclear. Here, by artificially transforming a previously neutral conspecific into an aggressive one, we show that valence updating depends on enhanced synaptic connectivity and physiological changes within the hippocampal ventral CA1 (vCA1)–basolateral amygdala (BLA)–nucleus accumbens (NAc) circuit. Following defeat, social memory engram neurons in the vCA1 strengthened their connections with BLA neurons carrying negative valence. The vCA1–BLA–NAc neural circuit flexibly regulates adaptive social behaviors.

title: The CARM1 epigenetic enzyme inhibits cross-presenting dendritic cell function in cancer immunity
abstract: The cancer-immunity cycle requires cross-presenting type I conventional dendritic cells (cDC1s) that induce T cell–mediated immunity, but therapeutic strategies for enhancing intratumoral cDC1 function are currently inadequate. We found the epigenetic enzyme CARM1 (coactivator-associated arginine methyltransferase 1) to be a selective negative regulator of cancer antigen presentation by cDC1s but not cDC2s. Inactivation of theCarm1gene promoted cDC1 antigen cross-presentation, activation, and accumulation in tumors, and a CARM1 inhibitor enhanced cDC1-mediated priming of T cells by means of a cancer neoantigen vaccine. CARM1 inhibition increased chromatin accessibility at BATF3-Jun and RelA sites that are critical for cDC1 function and activation. Transforming growth factor–β regulatedCarm1expression, which suggests that CARM1 inactivation enhanced intratumoral cDC1 function without altering cDC1 homeostasis. These studies identify CARM1 as a potential therapeutic target for enhancing the antitumor function of mouse and human cDC1s.

title: Leaping out of the water: Aerial-aquatic locomotion with flapping wings
abstract: Wing-propelled diving birds flap their wings to move through air and water, yet the wing morphology and kinematics that enable this behavior remain poorly understood because of the difficulty of collecting in situ data. The impact of flapping frequency, wing size, and stiffness on locomotion in—and transition between—the two media are still unknown. We compared data from diving birds against experiments using a flapping-wing robot capable of flying, swimming, plunge diving, and exiting the water. We show that frequency adaptation, flexible wings, and powerful actuation enable seamless transitions without folding wings or legs, that large wings enhance flight without substantially reducing underwater efficiency, and that tail-body distance and egress angle affect water exit. These results clarify how birds (and robots) balance multifluid locomotion constraints.
